# Transcrição: 20-Build Out Longtail Funnel

## [00:00 - 03:00]

Alright guys, let's talk about building out what we call long tail funnels so that we increase the results that we're getting from our ad campaigns for our clients. Now when you're first validating a funnel or getting things set up, you really focus mainly on the front end of the funnel. That is, you know, the ads that are going out to the cold audience, the landing pages, the email sequences that follow everything. And hopefully you've also set up some basic level of retargeting. So normally that would be, you know, people that viewed the landing page, people that opted in, and then people that added to cart. That would be like the basic level type of retargeting. Now once you've validated all of that and you've got all of that out of the way and it's performing well, it's hitting the KPIs that you want, there's one way to actually increase the results that you're getting and lower the cost per acquisition or cost per lead by building out a long-term, long tail funnel so extending our retargeting windows out and essentially in an evergreen manner. So people are seeing different messages and you're hitting different objectives and different pain points. So if we look at this kind of industry and this kind of funnel, the coaching and online courses, this is kind of a popular breakdown of a long tail funnel. So we're going to be retargeting people that hit the landing page, but they didn't opt in to our lead magnet essentially. So they clicked on our ad, they viewed the landing page and remember that the average landing page conversion rate is about 20%. So 80% of people showed an interest, but for whatever reason, maybe it was the landing page didn't do it for them. Maybe it was they were on their mobile, whatever it was, they bounced, they did not opt in, but they are a warm audience. They showed some level of interest. So we don't want to lose that because we spent money going after them, right? So we're going to actually set up some retargeting and break it down by 10, 20, 30 days. Now I just want to point out here guys that these days, these segments are a generalization, okay? This is kind of what we would do, but we do spend quite a bit on traffic and ad spend. If your client is only spending like $500 and you've only had 500 people hit that landing page, you don't want to segment it out too small. Like you may not want to do viewed lander five days. You may want to do viewed lander 20 days instead. So it's a bigger audience so that your ads feed out better. So just take that into consideration. Take into consideration how much your client is actually spending and how big are these audiences actually going to be. If you have audiences that are under sort of 100, 200 people, they're going to be very,

## [09:06 - 11:13]

consider the clients actual website. Most clients will have a website, right? For example, I have cat howl.com. And on that page, I have an FAQ section. I have, you know, different services or promotions or packages. I have an about me page. Now, if you have the time and if you want to get really granular about this, you should almost set up custom audiences based on specific pages people checked out. So for example, if someone checked out, you know, refund policy, FAQs or anything like that or who I am, I should be retargeting them with a personalized, not personalized, but, you know, a video of my personal story so that they get a better sense about who that I am so I can create more of a connection and a relationship with them. If they're checking out FAQs around pricing, I should invite them to speak to someone on my team to talk about, you know, what's best for them and different pricing options that they have. So think about like how to extend that funnel out even more and capture, recapture all those people that initially touched your funnel instead of just throwing it all to waste, right? If someone's clicked your ad at the top of the funnel, just because they didn't opt in right away and 80% on average will bounce right out, will not opt in, I should say. It doesn't mean you should just abandon them and keep going after more and more people, figure out ways to pull them right back into the funnel and then also extend your retargeting for people that have opted in or have booked a call with you so that you make the sales process easier, you make conversions on landing pages much higher, you build more of a relationship, more credibility, more trust using your funnel.
